text.title: AI Governance and Compliance — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `ai-governance-compliance` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: Risk-tier classification per EU AI Act is the first artefact. Other gates conditional on tier.
rule.rationale: Without tier classification, you can't know which gates are mandatory; mis-classification is the most expensive single mistake.

rule.statement: Every artefact carries a named DRI (role:person). Team-level ownership rejected.
rule.rationale: Anonymous artefacts decay; named DRI provides the accountability lawmakers expect.

rule.statement: Audit trail is append-only, hash-chained, and includes training data provenance + hyperparameter changes.
rule.rationale: Mutable audit trails fail regulatory audits; immutability is the minimum technical bar.

rule.statement: High-risk models include a fairness audit (Fairlearn / AIF360) AND an explainability artefact (SHAP / LIME).
rule.rationale: EU AI Act high-risk requires both; missing either fails the technical-file review.

rule.statement: High-risk decisions about people pass a human-review gate. Auto-approve forbidden for high-risk tiers.
rule.rationale: Auto-approve on high-risk decisions creates direct EU AI Act Article 14 violation.
